nodes.label:
Media
Coverage
nodes.title: Journalists amplify quantum computing announcements, often without technical scrutiny, creating an impression of imminent breakthroughs.

nodes.label:
Public
Excitement
nodes.title: Widespread media coverage generates public enthusiasm and a sense that quantum computing will transform industries soon.

nodes.label:
Political
Pressure
nodes.title: Excited constituents and industry lobbying push politicians to fund quantum initiatives to avoid falling behind rival nations.

nodes.label:
Government &
Corporate Funding
nodes.title: Political pressure and FOMO drive billions in public and private investment into quantum computing programs.

nodes.label:
Publications &
Press Releases
nodes.title: Funded labs produce papers and press releases announcing progress, often framed as breakthroughs regardless of practical significance.

nodes.label:
Bold
Claims
nodes.title: Press releases are distilled into bold, quotable claims about quantum supremacy, advantage, or commercial timelines.

nodes.label:
Skeptical
Analysis
nodes.title: Independent researchers and analysts examine claims critically, checking whether reported results have commercial relevance.

nodes.label:
Failed
Predictions
nodes.title: Over time, bold predictions that do not materialize accumulate as evidence against hype-driven timelines.

edges.title: Amplifies: Media coverage increases public excitement about quantum computing breakthroughs.
Triggers: Public excitement creates political pressure to fund quantum programs and not fall behind.
Funds: Political pressure drives government and corporate funding decisions.
Produces: Funding generates research output packaged as publications and press releases.
Generates: Press releases are distilled into bold, quotable claims about quantum milestones.
Amplifies: Bold claims become the raw material for the next round of media coverage.
Generates: Bold claims that fail to materialize become entries in the record of failed predictions.
Enables: A track record of failed predictions provides material for skeptical analysis.
Dampens: Skeptical analysis reduces public excitement by exposing the gap between claims and reality.
